source_quote: Deploy Agentic-Ready AI at the Edge with Memory Efficiency in NVIDIA JetPack 7.2

subject: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2支持内存优化的边缘智能体
object: With JetPack 7.2, Jetson is NemoClaw-ready out of the box. JetPack 7.2 comes preconfigured with the required dependencies and software stack, so you can deploy and run NemoClaw-based workflows on Jetson without manual environment setup. This enables you to easily build agentic physical AI applications across robotics, industrial automation, vision agents, and edge AI systems.
source_quote: With JetPack 7.2, Jetson is NemoClaw-ready out of the box. JetPack 7.2 comes preconfigured with the required dependencies and software stack, so you can deploy and run NemoClaw-based workflows on Jetson without manual environment setup. This enables you to easily build agentic physical AI applications across robotics, industrial automation, vision agents, and edge AI systems.

subject: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2支持内存优化的边缘智能体
object: Companies such as Konsulko Group and Peridio offer complete OS solutions like Konsulko Orca OS and Avocado OS , while Balena focuses on container-based fleet management and deployment at scale. Other NVIDIA partners include Neurealm , RidgeRun , and Wind River , who provide extensive engineering and NRE services with deep expertise in embedded Linux, BSP customization, multimedia pipelines, and long-term platform support. Together, this ecosystem enables developers to rapidly deploy, customize, and scale Yocto-based solutions on Jetson.
source_quote: Companies such as Konsulko Group and Peridio offer complete OS solutions like Konsulko Orca OS and Avocado OS , while Balena focuses on container-based fleet management and deployment at scale. Other NVIDIA partners include Neurealm , RidgeRun , and Wind River , who provide extensive engineering and NRE services with deep expertise in embedded Linux, BSP customization, multimedia pipelines, and long-term platform support. Together, this ecosystem enables developers to rapidly deploy, customize, and scale Yocto-based solutions on Jetson.

subject: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2支持内存优化的边缘智能体
object: JetPack 7.2 extends the Ubuntu 24.04, kernel 6.8 and CUDA Toolkit 13.0 -based compute stack (introduced with Jetson Thor) to the Jetson Orin family, bringing both platforms onto a single unified software foundation. With a common stack across Orin and Thor, you can seamlessly deploy the latest AI applications across the entire Jetson portfolio while taking advantage of the newest CUDA capabilities, libraries, and performance optimizations.
source_quote: JetPack 7.2 extends the Ubuntu 24.04, kernel 6.8 and CUDA Toolkit 13.0 -based compute stack (introduced with Jetson Thor) to the Jetson Orin family, bringing both platforms onto a single unified software foundation. With a common stack across Orin and Thor, you can seamlessly deploy the latest AI applications across the entire Jetson portfolio while taking advantage of the newest CUDA capabilities, libraries, and performance optimizations.

The company, which builds AI agents for enterprise customer service, has also surpassed $50 million in annual recurring revenue with […]

subject: Parloa
object: $350M with SAP, Microsoft, OpenAI partnerships
source_quote: Parloa, the Berlin-founded AI agent management platform, has announced a wave of strategic partnerships with SAP, Microsoft, OpenAI, Five9, and Epic as it deploys the $350 million it raised in its January 2026 Series D round. The company, which builds AI agents for enterprise customer service, has also surpassed $50 million in annual recurring revenue with 150% net revenue retention, according to its latest update.

subject: Parloa
object: $350M with SAP, Microsoft, OpenAI partnerships
source_quote: The company has also signed partnerships with Five9, the cloud contact centre provider, and Epic, the healthcare software company, to bring HIPAA-ready AI agents into clinical and patient support workflows. Outsourcing giants TP, Concentrix, and Foundever are deploying Parloa agents within their own operations, a sign that the business process outsourcing industry sees agentic AI as augmentation rather than replacement.

subject: Parloa
object: $350M with SAP, Microsoft, OpenAI partnerships
source_quote: Parloa’s pitch is that it sits at a different layer. Rather than being a contact centre platform that added AI, or an AI model provider that added customer service, Parloa positions itself as the management layer for deploying, testing, and optimising AI agents across whatever infrastructure an enterprise already runs. Its Agent Management Platform handles the full lifecycle from simulation to production, with what the company calls “build once, deploy anywhere” agent composition.

subject: Thoughtly
object: $8M+ to build omnichannel agents for revenue teams | Thoughtly
source_quote: Nomad, a national rental management platform, booked 650 meetings in 90 days, increased close rates on lower-score leads from 16% to 38%, and saved $400,000 in annual headcount costs — with an agent deployed within one week of signing. Nomad now runs 500–700 outbound calls per week and is expanding Thoughtly to handle its leasing support line, which processes over 13,000 monthly calls. "Speed-to-lead is the real game changer in sales. With Thoughtly, we're calling leads within 60 seconds of form submission. That's just not possible with a human team," said Will O'Neil, Head of IT & People at Nomad

subject: Thoughtly
object: $8M+ to build omnichannel agents for revenue teams | Thoughtly
source_quote: NEW YORK, April 22, 2026 — Thoughtly, the AI engagement platform to give CRMs a voice, today announced a $5.5 million Seed round, bringing total funding to $8+ million. The round was led by Armory Square Ventures, with participation from nvp capital, Converge, K5 Global Tokyo Black , and returning investors Afore Capital and Greycroft.

subject: Thoughtly
object: $8M+ to build omnichannel agents for revenue teams | Thoughtly
source_quote: The announcement coincides with the launch of Thoughtly's omnichannel platform, which expands beyond voice to include SMS and email — allowing revenue teams to reach every lead, on every channel, from a single CRM-native system.

subject: Thoughtly
object: $8M+ to build omnichannel agents for revenue teams | Thoughtly
source_quote: Most revenue teams can only human-touch a fraction of their pipeline. The rest go cold. Email and SMS drip campaigns underperform. And while teams have automated data workflows and digital channels, phone conversations have remained largely manual — creating coverage gaps, slower response times, and missed revenue.

source_quote: Dakotah Rice is stepping back into the founder’s seat. On Wednesday, he announced that his new company, the AI-native insurance brokerage Harper, has raised $46.8 million in a combined Series A and seed round.

subject: Y Combinator grad and AI insurance brokerage Harper
source_quote: Investors in the company include YC, Lobster Capital, and Peak XV Partners, and its Series A round was led by Emergence Capital.

subject: Y Combinator grad and AI insurance brokerage Harper
source_quote: For his next company, he went back to his roots. His family owned an insurance brokerage, and he remembered all the hassles that came when founders like himself walked through the doors, trying to insure their businesses. “I hated insurance,” he continued, “swore I’d never end up in it.”

subject: Telli, a YC alum,
object: pre-seed funding for its AI voice agents
source_quote: Former Y Combinator startup Telli is helping companies alleviate the bottleneck that occurs when a high-volume of customers try to, for example, book appointments. Its AI voice agents kick in and handle basic operations while handing off more-complex processes to human operators. The Berlin-based startup has now raised $3.6 million in a pre-seed funding round led by Berlin’s Cherry Ventures and Y Combinator.

source_quote: Elon Musk’s xAI announced a $20 billion Series E round and Sam Altman’s brain computer interface startup Merge Labs raised a $250 million seed round (with OpenAI as the lead investor) in the first few weeks of 2026, so signs point to another strong year. Of course, it’s still early; we’ll be watching to see if the momentum continues.

subject: OpenAI
object: a way for enterprises to build and manage AI agents
source_quote: On Thursday, AI giant OpenAI announced the launch of OpenAI Frontier , an end-to-end platform designed for enterprises to build and manage AI agents. It’s an open platform, which means users can manage agents built outside of OpenAI too.

subject: OpenAI
object: a way for enterprises to build and manage AI agents
source_quote: It’s not surprising that OpenAI would release this platform in early 2026, as the company has made it clear that enterprise adoption is one of its main focus areas for this year . The company has also announced two notable enterprise deals this year with ServiceNow and Snowflake.

subject: Empromptu
object: $2M pre-seed to help enterprises build AI apps
source_quote: After CodeSee was acquired in 2024, Leven decided that she wanted to build a product that would let business owners, even those without technical backgrounds, build AI applications. She teamed up with AI researcher Sean Robinson, and last October, the two launched Empromptu, an AI service that businesses can use to build AI applications.

subject: Relevance AI

source_quote: Relevance AI raises $24M to help businesses build AI agents

subject: Relevance AI
object: $24M to help businesses build AI agents
source_quote: Relevance AI , a San Francisco- and Sydney-based startup developing an AI agent “operating system” to enable businesses to build teams of AI agents, has raised $24 million in Series B funding led by Bessemer Venture Partners. Returning investors King River Capital, Insight Partners, and Peak XV also participated, bringing Relevance’s total raised to $37 million. The company did not provide its valuation.

subject: Relevance AI
object: $24M to help businesses build AI agents
source_quote: The fundraising comes about a year and a half after the startup closed its Series A . Relevance says that it has experienced rapid growth, with 40,000 AI agents registered on its platform in January 2025 alone.

subject: Relevance AI
object: $24M to help businesses build AI agents
source_quote: Coinciding with its Series B funding, Relevance is introducing two new features on its platform. “Workforce” is a no-code multi-agent system designed to help non-technical professionals and engineers build specialized teams of agents to collaborate like human employees, completing complex processes from start to finish. “Invent” is a tool that lets users create AI agents using text prompts.
